question_text: Which of the following is the primary source of energy stored in food?

options.label: A
options.text: Energy from the Sun captured during photosynthesis

options.label: B
options.text: Energy released from the Earth's core

options.label: C
options.text: Energy produced by decomposition of minerals

options.label: D
options.text: Energy generated by friction in the atmosphere

correct_answer: Energy from the Sun captured during photosynthesis
explanation: Plants use photosynthesis to capture light energy from the Sun and convert it into chemical energy stored in food molecules. When animals eat plants (or other animals), they obtain this stored energy, which is passed along food chains. This is the primary source of energy in nearly all food on Earth.
